data.narrative:
Transmutex is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $23M to date. The sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers spanning early-stage through later-stage development, including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $23M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $46M. This reflects typical step-up economics from Series A to subsequent rounds. Peer-median triangulation could not be applied: while 8 nuclear energy comps exist in the dataset, none of the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median pricing. A sector-wide median would introduce excessive dispersion given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology approaches and development timelines.

The single applicable method supports a valuation range of $39M (low) to $53M (high), with a $46M median. Given reliance on one triangulation method, key uncertainties include timing to Series B fundraising, capital intensity of reactor licensing and deployment, and technology risk relative to competing modular approaches in the peer set. The narrow analytical foundation warrants conservative weighting of this range pending expanded comparable company data or market activity.
